Off Calendar Oct-30-2013 Continued To Nov-13-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (461 27th Street, S.F.)
Off Calendar Oct-10-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Lofdahl for and with Diane Gomez-Rigby (Administrator). Tara Donohue from Zephyr Real Estate was also present.

The Court raised the issue of an offer being accepted prior to publicaiton. Ms. Donohue stated that many Parties were interested in the property, but that no additional offers were received. The sale was confirmed to Tim Graffigna (Agent) on behalf of Robert Poynter and Nili Poynter at $1,123,000.00 subject to the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed August 14, 2013. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to receipt and review of the order). The Court will allow $17,000.00 out of escrow to be deposited into the Estate account at Bank of America. The remaining balance of the proceeds (estimated amount of net proceeds $1,063,000.00) are to be deposited into four separate banks in blocked accounts with no more than $250,000.00 in each account. Status on filing of four receipts for the blocked accounts was set for October 30, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-11-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Lofdahl for and with Diane Gomez-Rigby. Paulette Gomez (Daughter of Decedent) also present.

Ms. Lofdahl stated that they were still asking for her Client to have limited authority for the house but that there is a bank account at Bank of America (amount unknown) and that they were now asking for a bond in the amount of $20,000.00. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with limited authority and bond in the amount of $20,000.00 (subject to review of the order). Ms. Lofdahl submitted a revised order reflecting the bond amount.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.
